description_long: This is a vintage triplane with three stacked, straight wings and a single wooden propeller mounted at the nose. The fuselage is slender and tapers toward the tail, which features a conventional tail assembly with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The aircraft is painted in a light tan color with roundel insignias on the wings and fuselage, and the cockpit is open and positioned just behind the upper wing. The propeller is a distinct reddish-brown wood, and the engine cowling is metallic. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century military biplanes used in World War I.
description: A vintage tan triplane with three stacked wings, a wooden propeller, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear, featuring roundel insignias.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three stacked wings, wooden propeller, and fixed main landing gear.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the roundel insignias on the wings and the open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, roundels on both upper wings, and the cockpit placement.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the tail section, single vertical stabilizer, and the fixed landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Triplane configuration with three straight, stacked wings in a mid-wing arrangement; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing in a forward-mid fuselage position.
